title: Group IID, IIE, IIF and III secreted phospholipase A<sub>2</sub>s.
author_string: Murakami, Makoto, Miki, Yoshimi, Sato, Hiroyasu, Murase, Remi, Taketomi, Yoshitaka et al.
journal: Biochim Biophys Acta Mol Cell Biol Lipids
journal_full: Biochimica et biophysica acta. Molecular and cell biology of lipids
abstract: Among the 11 members of the secreted phospholipase A<sub>2</sub> (sPLA<sub>2</sub>) family, group IID, IIE, IIF and III sPLA<sub>2</sub>s (sPLA<sub>2</sub>-IID, -IIE, -IIF and -III, respectively) are "new" isoforms in the history of sPLA<sub>2</sub> research. Relative to the better characterized sPLA<sub>2</sub>s (sPLA<sub>2</sub>-IB, -IIA, -V and -X), the enzymatic properties, distributions, and functions of these "new" sPLA<sub>2</sub>s have remained obscure until recently. Our current studies using knockout and transgenic mice for a nearly full set of sPLA<sub>2</sub>s, in combination with comprehensive lipidomics, have revealed unique and distinct roles of these "new" sPLA<sub>2</sub>s in specific biological events. Thus, sPLA<sub>2</sub>-IID is involved in immune suppression, sPLA<sub>2</sub>-IIE in metabolic regulation and hair follicle homeostasis, sPLA<sub>2</sub>-IIF in epidermal hyperplasia, and sPLA<sub>2</sub>-III in male reproduction, anaphylaxis, colonic diseases, and possibly atherosclerosis. In this article, we overview current understanding of the properties and functions of these sPLA<sub>2</sub>s and their underlying lipid pathways in vivo.
